sourceSummary.oneLine: Configuration file identifying Bio-Formats file format readers and the order in which they should be used.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines available Bio-Formats reader classes, associated file extensions, third-party library dependencies, disabled readers, and ordering constraints for fallback readers.
keyPoints: The document states it identifies all file format readers available to Bio-Formats and their usage order.
ScreenReader for HCS data is disabled because it caused problems outside OMERO.
Standard PIC, TIFF, and text readers are required to go last because they accept broad file classes.
Some readers require third-party libraries such as POI, JAI-ImageIO, NetCDF, MDB Tools, and LuraWave.

entities.name: OMERO
entities.evidence: disabled for now, as it has been causing no end of problems outside of OMERO

concepts.name: Bio-Formats reader configuration
concepts.evidence: This document is a configuration file identifying all file format readers available to Bio-Formats, and the order in which they should be used.

concepts.name: Reader ordering constraints
concepts.evidence: standard PIC reader must go last; standard TIFF reader must go last; standard text reader must go last

concepts.name: Disabled HCS ScreenReader
concepts.evidence: generic reader for HCS data; disabled for now

concepts.name: TIFF-based readers
concepts.evidence: TIFF-based readers with unique file extensions; TIFF-based readers with slow isThisType

evidence.claim: The source is a configuration file for Bio-Formats file format readers and their order of use.
evidence.evidenceText: This document is a configuration file identifying all file format readers available to Bio-Formats, and the order in which they should be used.

evidence.claim: ScreenReader is a generic reader for HCS data but is disabled in this configuration.
evidence.evidenceText: generic reader for HCS data; disabled for now, as it has been causing no end of problems outside of OMERO; #loci.formats.in.ScreenReader

evidence.claim: ZipReader is configured for compressed/archive zip files.
evidence.evidenceText: readers for compressed/archive files; loci.formats.in.ZipReader # zip

evidence.claim: APNGReader and JPEGReader are javax.imageio readers.
evidence.evidenceText: javax.imageio readers; loci.formats.in.APNGReader # png [javax.imageio]; loci.formats.in.JPEGReader # jpg, jpeg [javax.imageio]

evidence.claim: Some Bio-Formats readers require third-party libraries.
evidence.evidenceText: readers requiring third-party libraries; FV1000Reader # oib, oif, various [POI]; JPEG2000Reader # jp2, j2k [JAI-ImageIO]; ImarisHDFReader # ims [NetCDF]

evidence.claim: The standard PIC reader must be ordered last because it accepts any PIC file.
evidence.evidenceText: standard PIC reader must go last (it accepts any PIC); loci.formats.in.BioRadReader # pic

evidence.claim: The standard TIFF reader must be ordered last because it accepts any TIFF file.
evidence.evidenceText: standard TIFF reader must go last (it accepts any TIFF); loci.formats.in.TiffDelegateReader # tif, tiff

evidence.claim: The standard text reader must be ordered last because it accepts any plaintext.
evidence.evidenceText: standard text reader must go last (it accepts any plaintext); loci.formats.in.TextReader # txt, csv

reviewFlags.reason: No document title, revision/version, document date, regulatory authority, jurisdiction, or standards are present in the source text.
Source appears to be a software configuration file rather than an RA/regulatory document.
Authority signal is general and no official authority is identified in the source text.
